title: Love and Friendship by Emily Bronte — Love Poetry Lines
content:
Love is like the wild rose-briar,
Friendship like the holly-tree --
The holly is dark when the rose-briar blooms
But which will bloom most contantly?
The wild-rose briar is sweet in the spring,
Its summer blossoms scent the air;
Yet wait till winter comes again
And who wil call the wild-briar fair?
Then scorn the silly rose-wreath now
And deck thee with the holly's sheen,
That when December blights thy brow
He may still leave thy garland green.
meaning: "Love and Friendship" by Emily Bronte is a love and nature poem consisting of 12 lines. The poet explores themes of love and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Love is like the wild rose-briar, Friendship like the holly-tree --...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Bronte's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
